consumer_electronics.title: Asus chases Elgato with its own secondary touchscreen display
consumer_electronics.summary: Asus's latest gaming monitor is a little smaller than usual. The ROG Strix XG129C, announced on Friday, is a 12.3-inch touchscreen IPS display that's intended to be a sidekick for a larger main monitor, similar to the 14.1-inch secondary display in the 2020 Asus ROG Zephyrus Duo 15. consumer_electronics.title: Amazon is adding a vertical video feed to Prime Video
consumer_electronics.summary: Amazon Prime Video is joining Netflix and Disney Plus in adding a vertical feed of videos to its streaming app. The new "Clips" feed will offer a stream of short-form videos from shows and movies, with options to jump into the full title, rent, or buy it. Prime Video previously offered a TikTok-style feed with […

consumer_electronics.title: Here is Yarbo’s promise to fix the robot mower that ran me over
consumer_electronics.summary: Yesterday, I told you how a hacker ran me over with a robot lawn mower. We explained how thousands of these bladed Chinese robots, made by Yarbo, could be hijacked with ease - exposing people's GPS coordinates, Wi-Fi passwords, email addresses, and more to any casual hacker who comes along. consumer_electronics.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
consumer_electronics.summary: Massive new data centers are the physical foundation for tech companies’ hopes and dreams for AI. consumer_electronics.title: DOGE used ChatGPT in a way that was both dumb and illegal, judge rules
consumer_electronics.summary: The Department of Government Efficiency's cancellation of over $100 million in grants was unconstitutional, according to a ruling on Thursday. consumer_electronics.title: Apple reportedly has a deal to use Intel-made chips again

consumer_electronics.title: San Francisco’s housing market has lost its mind

San Francisco is home to some of the most valuable private companies in the world, and their employees have been quietly accumulating — and, increasingly, cashing out — fortunes.

consumer_electronics.title: Porsche shutters e-bike, battery, software subsidiaries as part of company overhaul
consumer_electronics.summary: More than 500 people will be affected by the closures. “We must refocus on our core business,” Porsche CEO and executive chair Michael Leiters said in a statement.
